skill.name: production-readiness
skill.description: Comprehensive pre-deployment validation ensuring code is production-ready. Runs complete audit pipeline, performance benchmarks, security scan, documentation check, and generates deployment checklist.
skill.summary: Comprehensive pre-deployment validation ensuring code is production-ready. Runs complete audit pipel...
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, filesystem, environment, Windows SAM, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found no prompt injection, no Windows SAM access, no weak cryptography implementation, and no confirmed malicious intent. The skill does instruct users to run a broad shell workflow, including npx tooling and recursive secret keyword scans, so it should publish with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill provides a bash workflow that runs npx claude-flow commands, npm audit, grep, jq, and shell arithmetic against a user-supplied target path. This is legitimate for production readiness checks, but it executes local tooling and should be run only in trusted repositories or disposable environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is explicit and central to the skill. The commands support the stated purpose, and I found no evidence of command injection or malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Secret Keyword Grep Can Print Sensitive Matches
medium_findings.description: The workflow recursively searches for api_key, password, secret, and token strings and prints matching lines. This can expose real secrets in terminal output or AI transcripts, even though the intent is to detect hardcoded credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The grep pipeline is directly visible and prints matching source lines by default. This is a real exposure risk, but I found no evidence of network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Writes and Checks
low_findings.description: The workflow creates a production-readiness output directory, writes a deployment checklist, and checks for documentation and environment example files. These operations are expected for the skill, but users should review generated files before committing them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are explicit and local. I found no evidence that the skill reads secret file contents or writes outside its report directory.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive-File and Weak-Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Windows SAM and weak cryptography detections do not correspond to actual SAM database access or cryptographic code. The referenced lines contain deployment status variables, prose, cascade references, or failure mode text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed directly and do not access Windows SAM files or implement cryptography. This strongly supports dismissing those static matches.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx-Based Tool Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow calls npx claude-flow subcommands. npx can execute installed or registry-provided package code, creating a supply-chain and local execution risk if the environment is not controlled.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx invocations are explicit. They are not malicious by themselves, but they execute external package tooling as part of the workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Source Scanning for Secret Terms
dangerous_patterns.description: The grep command scans the target path for credential-related words and can emit matching lines. This can disclose sensitive strings to local logs or assistant context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and its output behavior are clear from the source. The risk is local disclosure rather than remote exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Deployment risk grows when teams skip quality, security, performance, and documentation checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured release gate and checklist.
actual_capabilities: Defines quality gates for tests, coverage, code quality, security, performance, documentation, and rollback readiness.
Provides a bash workflow for running audit, security, dependency, performance, and documentation checks.
Generates a deployment checklist with sign-off sections and rollback steps.
Checks common project files such as README, deployment documentation, rollback documentation, and package.json.
Summarizes go or no-go deployment status based on gate results.
limitations: Requires local command-line tools such as npx, jq, npm, and claude-flow to be available.
Does not replace human release approval, incident planning, or infrastructure review.
Performance checks are generic and may not match every service-level objective.
Secret scanning uses simple keyword matching and can produce false positives or expose matching lines.

use_cases.title: Release Manager Gate Review
use_cases.description: Run a consistent release readiness review before approving a production deployment.

use_cases.title: Developer Pre-Merge Validation
use_cases.description: Check tests, coverage, security results, and documentation before handing work to operations.

use_cases.title: DevOps Deployment Checklist
use_cases.description: Create a structured checklist for staging validation, production rollout, monitoring, and rollback.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Readiness Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production-readiness skill to review this repository for a production release. Focus on tests, security, documentation, and blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Staging Release Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production-readiness skill for a staging deployment review. Note any warnings that can wait and any issues that must block release.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Release Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production-readiness skill with strict security review. Prioritize secrets, dependency vulnerabilities, unsafe patterns, and rollback readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Full Production Go Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the production-readiness skill to produce a final go or no-go decision for production. Include gate results, blockers, warnings, and required approvals.

output_examples.input: Review my service before a production deployment.
output: Blocking issues: failing tests, missing rollback plan, and unresolved high dependency vulnerability.
Next step: fix blockers, rerun validation, then request release sign-off.

output_examples.input: Create a deployment checklist for a staging release.
output: Checklist includes quality gates, security review, performance checks, documentation review, monitoring, deployment steps, and rollback tasks.
Staging warnings are separated from production blockers.

output_examples.input: Assess production readiness after all tests pass.
output: Tests passed, but readiness still depends on coverage, security results, documentation, performance, and rollback evidence.
The final decision should list remaining risks and required approvals.

best_practices: Run the workflow in a clean checkout or disposable environment before release.
Review secret scan output carefully and redact sensitive values before sharing results.
Adjust quality gates and performance thresholds to match the service being deployed.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a generated checklist as final approval without human review.
Do not run the command workflow on untrusted repositories without sandboxing.
Do not paste secret scan output into shared channels without redaction.

faq.question: What does this skill check?
faq.answer: It checks tests, code quality, coverage, security, dependencies, documentation, performance, monitoring, and rollback readiness.

faq.question: Can it deploy my application?
faq.answer: No. It prepares a readiness assessment and checklist, but deployment execution remains a separate process.

faq.question: Does it require command-line tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow expects tools such as npx, npm, jq, grep, and claude-flow.

faq.question: Is the security scan comprehensive?
faq.answer: No. It combines tool output and simple checks, but teams should still use dedicated security testing.

faq.question: Can I use it for staging?
faq.answer: Yes. The input contract includes staging and production modes, with production using stricter expectations.

faq.question: What should I do with failed gates?
faq.answer: Treat failed gates as release blockers unless your team documents an explicit exception and mitigation.
